name: 梁振伟
current_positions: Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth
Senior Minister of State for Education
Member of Parliament (Tampines North, Tampines GRC)

education.institution: Indonesian Army Command and General Staff College (SESKOAD)

work_experience.position: Commando officer; rose to Major-General and Chief of Army (2022-2025)

work_experience.organization: Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth

work_experience.position: Member of Parliament (Tampines North, Tampines GRC)

person_relationships.description: Part of PAP team for Tampines GRC in GE2025, alongside Masagos Zulkifli
Succeeded Goh Si Hou as Chief of Army in March 2022
Succeeded Edwin Tong as Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth in May 2025

political_stances.stance_content: As Acting Minister for MCCY, chairs the National Integration Council, Singapore Cares Steering Committee, National Youth Council, and National Steering Committee on Racial and Religious Harmony
Drew criticism for calling Hong Kong football fans 'idiots' after Singapore's 2-1 win in Asian Cup qualifiers at Kai Tak Stadium

major_achievements.achievement: Became Chief of Army; first Commando officer to hold this position
Led SAF's contributions to national COVID-19 fight as Head of Joint Plans and Director of Joint Operations
Programme director of Pioneer Generation Office and founding group chief of Silver Generation Office under Agency for Integrated Care
Awarded Public Administration Medal (Gold) (Military) and Indonesian Army Meritorious Service Star, 1st Class
Received Knight Grand Cross of the Order of the Crown of Thailand and Gold Cross of Honour of the German Armed Forces
Appointed Acting Minister for MCCY and Senior Minister of State for Education in first electoral term; one of two new MPs promoted directly to Acting Minister

biography_summary: David Neo Chin Wee (born 17 September 1977) is a Singaporean politician and retired Major-General who has been serving as Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth, and Senior Minister of State for Education since May 2025. Raised by his uncle and aunt after his mother died when he was five, he attended Victoria School and Victoria Junior College before earning degrees in Electrical Engineering and Economics from the University of Illinois, an MS from Stanford, and an MBA from MIT under Lee Kuan Yew Scholarship. He served in the SAF from 1996, rising to Chief of Army (2022-2025) as the first Commando to hold the role. He oversaw SAF's COVID-19 operations and was programme director of the Pioneer Generation Office. He entered politics in GE2025, winning Tampines GRC with 52.02% of the vote, and was immediately appointed Acting Minister.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with detailed military career (first Commando Chief of Army), education specifics (dual degree UIUC, Stanford MS, MIT MBA, SESKOAD), COVID-19 contributions, Pioneer Generation Office role, GE2025 results, MCCY chairmanships, and controversy.
